What is your main concern right now?

care_services.name: Memory & Dementia Care (Intensive)

care_services.description: Brief visits or check-ins to prompt, organize, and document medication intake.

care_services.description: Light housekeeping, laundry, meal prep, and errands to maintain a safe home environment.

care_services.name: Phone Consultation

care_services.description: Guided online questionnaire that collects health, safety, and support needs to build a care plan.

articles.title: Room-by-Room Home Safety Checklist to Prevent Falls
articles.summary: Walk through each room of the home with this practical checklist to spot and fix common fall hazards.
articles.content: Falls are the leading cause of injury for older adults, but many risks can be reduced with simple home modifications. In this room-by-room checklist, we cover flooring, lighting, furniture placement, bathroom grab bars, and stair safety so you can confidently assess and improve your loved ones environment.

articles.title: Bathroom Fall Hazards: 7 Fixes You Can Do This Weekend
articles.summary: From non-slip mats to lighting upgrades, learn quick changes that dramatically lower bathroom fall risk.
articles.content: Bathrooms are one of the most common places for serious falls. This article outlines seven high-impact, low-effort changesincluding grab bars, raised toilet seats, non-slip mats, and proper towel bar placementto help keep your loved one safe without a major remodel.

articles.title: Nighttime Fall Prevention: Safer Trips to the Bathroom
articles.summary: Simple lighting, layout, and routine changes that reduce the risk of nighttime falls.
articles.content: Many falls happen at night, when vision is reduced and medications may cause grogginess. We share practical tips on path lighting, bed height, footwear, and hydration routines to support safe nighttime bathroom trips and better sleep.

articles.title: Outdoor Fall Risks: Making Steps and Walkways Safer
articles.summary: How to reduce fall risk on outdoor steps, porches, and uneven walkways.
articles.content: Outdoor spaces can be especially risky due to uneven concrete, weather, and poor lighting. Learn how to improve railings, step visibility, textures, and seasonal maintenance so your loved one can enjoy the outdoors more safely.

articles.title: Essential Fall Prevention Guide for Family Caregivers
articles.summary: A comprehensive overview of fall risk factors and prevention strategies for seniors living at home.
articles.content: This in-depth guide explains intrinsic and extrinsic risk factors for falls, how to talk with your loved one about safety, and when to consider professional in-home support. While some data reference 2024 statistics, the core strategies remain relevant today.

articles.title: Managing Diabetes at Home: Monitoring, Meals, and Meds
articles.summary: Key routines and tools that make diabetes care at home safer and more manageable.
articles.content: We cover blood glucose monitoring, meal planning, foot checks, and medication routines to help families feel more confident managing diabetes at home, including when to call a nurse or provider.

articles.title: Caregiver Burnout: 8 Warning Signs and How to Get Support
articles.summary: Learn to recognize caregiver burnout early and explore options for respite and professional help.
articles.content: Caring for a loved one is meaningful but demanding. This article explains emotional, physical, and behavioral signs of burnout, and offers steps for setting boundaries, asking for help, and integrating home nursing services.

articles.title: First Week After Knee Replacement: What to Expect at Home
articles.summary: Day-by-day guidance for the first week after knee replacement surgery, including pain, mobility, and wound care.
articles.content: We outline a typical recovery timeline, how to prepare the home, safe transfer techniques, and what a post-surgery nursing visit can help with, including wound checks and medication management.

articles.title: Designing Calming Daily Routines for Dementia Care
articles.summary: Structure and activities that can reduce agitation and confusion for people living with dementia.
articles.content: Consistent routines can lower anxiety for people with dementia. We discuss morning, afternoon, and evening structure, how to cue activities, and when dementia-focused home visits may help stabilize routines.

articles.title: Medication Management at Home: Pillboxes, Apps, and Reminders
articles.summary: Compare simple and tech-enabled tools that keep complex medication schedules on track.
articles.content: From color-coded pillboxes to smartphone reminders and professional medication reminder visits, this article walks through options to prevent missed or double doses and keep your loved ones regimen organized.

assessment_options.text: Preventing falls and safety at home
Recovering after a hospital stay or surgery
Memory loss, confusion, or dementia behaviors
Needs a lot of assistance or uses a lift
Lives with adult children or other family
Lives in a senior community or assisted living

Post-knee-replacement check; has stair lift at home.
Family requested afternoons due to sleep pattern.
Rescheduled from March 10; prefers consistent afternoon routine.

care_plans.name: Overnight Companion Care  Value Plan (2 Nights/Week)
care_plans.description: Affordable overnight companion support two nights per week for clients who need supervision and reassurance but minimal hands-on nursing care.

care_plans.name: Overnight Companion Care  Budget Plan (3 Nights/Week)
care_plans.description: Our most budget-friendly 3-night-per-week overnight companion package, ideal for families seeking regular nighttime coverage under a set budget.

care_plans.name: Overnight Companion Care  Extended Plan (33+ Nights/Week)
care_plans.description: Flexible extended overnight coverage for families who need 3 to 5 nights of non-medical supervision and support each week.

care_plans.name: Overnight Nursing Care  Standard Plan (3 Nights/Week)
care_plans.description: Overnight visits from a licensed nurse three nights per week, including vitals monitoring, medication management, and hands-on clinical care.

care_plans.name: Overnight Nursing Care  Premium Plan (57 Nights/Week)
care_plans.description: High-frequency overnight nursing for complex medical needs, with up to nightly coverage and priority scheduling.

care_plans.name: Memory & Dementia Care  Basic (3 Visits/Week)
care_plans.description: Three structured dementia-focused visits per week supporting routines, cueing, safety, and caregiver education.

care_plans.name: Memory & Dementia Care  Plus (4 Visits/Week)
care_plans.description: Four dementia care visits per week for clients who benefit from near-daily structure, redirection, and safety monitoring.

care_plans.name: Memory & Dementia Care  Intensive (5 Visits/Week)
care_plans.description: Five weekly intensive dementia care visits focused on behavior management, wandering prevention, and close caregiver coaching.

care_plans.name: Custom Weekly Care Plan (Up to 10 Hours/Week)
care_plans.description: Flexible mix of personal care, medication reminders, and household support totaling around 10 hours per week, tailored during your consultation.
